descriptionShort: The first voicebank produced by INTERNET Co., Gakupo was also the first Vocaloid to be voiced by a major Japanese singer. Three updated VOCALOID3 voicebanks have since been developed for him. These VOCALOID3 voicebanks have went on to be updated for the VOCALOID4 Engine, each fea
name: Camui Gackpo

descriptionShort: This artist prohibits reprints.
name: Danjou Sora

descriptionShort: 岩清水P claims that EasyPop is their elder brother in their uploads to EasyPop's channel. Whether this is true or not is unclear.
name: EasyPop

descriptionShort:
* Female 1 is the voice behind [Yukkuri Reimu](https://vocadb.net/T/9432). As such, it's decently popular to pair her with "[女声2](https://vocadb.net/Ar/104088)", who plays the role of [Yukkuri Marisa](https://vocadb.net/T/9433).
* Due to [Taberungo no Uta](https://vocadb.net/S/27
name: Female Voice 1 (AquesTalk)

descriptionShort: Note: Giga's producer tag is [Kagamine Len (鏡音レン)](https://vocadb.net/Ar/15) and occasionally [Kagamine Rin (鏡音レン)](https://vocadb.net/Ar/14) saying "Giga"; although this alone does not qualify such songs for an entry.
name: Giga

descriptionShort: The second voicebank produced by INTERNET Co., GUMI is notable for being one of the most natural-sounding Vocaloids right out of the box. She has since received four updated VOCALOID3 voicebanks: Sweet, Power, Adult, and Whisper. In addition, INTERNET has released a V3 update of
name: GUMI

descriptionShort: Is the same person as [P*Light](https://vocadb.net/Ar/252).
name: HachioujiP

descriptionShort: Hatsune Miku (初音ミク) is a singing synthesizer application with a female persona, developed by Crypton Future Media. It uses Yamaha Corporation's Vocaloid 2 synthesizing technology. The name of the character comes from a fusion of the Japanese for first (初 hatsu), sound (音 ne) and
name: Hatsune Miku

descriptionShort: 1 of 6 VOCALOID2 Miku Appends.
name: Hatsune Miku Append (Dark)

descriptionShort: This artist is used as a replacement when it is known that the song uses Miku NT, but it's not known which voicebank. Avoid using this artist if you can find out the specific voicebank.
name: Hatsune Miku NT (Unknown)

descriptionShort: One of Miku's 7 Japanese V3 voicebanks.
name: Hatsune Miku V3 (Soft)

descriptionShort: Whisper voicebank of Hatsune Miku V4X. Includes breath release but no voice colours.
name: Hatsune Miku V4X (Dark)

descriptionShort: Solid voicebank of Hatsune Miku V4X. Includes voice colours Power and Soft, as well as breath release.
name: Hatsune Miku V4X (Solid)

name: HoneyWorks

descriptionShort: The first voicebank developed by 1st PLACE. Her voice provider, Lia, is best known for singing ["Tori no Uta"](https://vocadb.net/S/165696), the opening theme of the visual novel, *Air*. IA's name is pronounced "EE-ah" (rhyming with "Lia").
name: IA

additionalNames: 音楽的同位体 可不
name: KAFU

descriptionShort: Kagamine Len, alongside [Kagamine Rin](http://vocadb.net/Ar/14), is the second entry in Crypton's VOCALOID2 Character Vocal Series. As the fifth voicebank released by Crypton, Kagamine Len has since received an act2, Append, and a VOCALOID4 update.
name: Kagamine Len

descriptionShort: Kagamine Rin, alongside [Kagamine Len](http://vocadb.net/Ar/15), is the second entry in Crypton's VOCALOID2 Character Vocal Series. As the fourth voicebank released by Crypton, Kagamine Rin has since received an act2, Append, and a VOCALOID4 update.
name: Kagamine Rin

descriptionShort: KAITO was created as the male counterpart to [MEIKO](http://vocadb.net/Ar/176); both were designed to provide professional vocals for professional musicians.
name: KAITO

descriptionShort: Shouta is a talented musician born in the Heisei era, raised in the digital world. He's a multi-player who can handle various instruments such as piano, guitar, and bass.
name: kemu

descriptionShort: **Use this artist when it's unsure which version of Koharu Rikka is being used**.
name: Koharu Rikka (Unknown)

descriptionShort:
Recommended Range: B1 ~ E3
Recommended Tempo: 70 ~ 210 BPM
name: KYO

descriptionShort: The third entry in Crypton's Character Vocals Series, and the fifth voicebank produced by Crypton. Luka was the first Vocaloid with bilingual capability.
name: Megurine Luka

descriptionShort: Soft library featuring E.V.E.C. capabilities.
name: Megurine Luka V4X (Soft)

descriptionShort: Megurine Luka's VOCALOID4 update; the first of Crypton Future Media's products to feature E.V.E.C. (Enhanced Voice Expression Control).
name: Megurine Luka V4X (Unknown)

descriptionShort:
A VOCALOID bundled with [Meika Hime](https://vocadb.net/Ar/71655).
They also have a talk software called [Gynoid Talk](https://vocadb.net/Ar/71658).
name: Meika Mikoto

name: MEIKO

A circle that began in 2013 and consists of Sakurai and Kimura.
Sakurai does the lyrics, composition, tuning, and drums, while Kimura does the synthesis, keyboard and guitar.

descriptionShort: Produced as the first entry in AH-Software's Artist Vocal series. Miki was created to have a wide range and to sound greatly different depending on the pitch of her voice. She has one of the largest optimum vocal ranges among the VOCALOID2 voicebanks.
name: SF-A2 Codename miki

descriptionShort:

She isn't one to hold back her thoughts, so she basically speaks to everyone as though they were her friends.
name: Shikoku Metan

The VOCALOID4 update of v flower.
Range: F#2 ~ F4
Tempo: 80 ~ 200BPM

name: VY2

descriptionShort: Yuzuki Yukari is the first voicebank developed for VOCALOID3 by AH-Software and VOCALOMAKETS. She is the first Vocaloid to also have a Voiceroid component, able to synthesize speech as well as singing; her voicebank is aimed at producers rather than at the general Vocaloid fandom
name: Yuzuki Yukari

descriptionShort:
